head.hi: The Consul General at Shanghai (Cunningham) to the Secretary of State

div.p: 99. Referring to Department’s telegram No. 43, February 20, 5 p.m., and in continuation of my 64, February 15, 4 p.m. Third report of League of Nations Shanghai Committee was drafted February 20.For text of third report, see League of Nations, Official Journal, March 1932, p. 382. Report covers arrangements for the meeting of the Chinese and Japanese military on the 18th, unsatisfactory demands of the Japanese in regard to evacuation, the delivery at 9 p.m. to the Mayor and commander of the 19th Route Army of written Japanese demands (see my 82, February 19, 7 p.m.), summary of the replies by the Mayor to the Japanese Consul General and by the Chinese commander to the Japanese commander (see my 83, February 19, 10 a.m. [p.m.]). The report states that no reply has been received by the Japanese forces. Final paragraph of report is only important finding which stated that “during the night 19th, 20th Japanese reenforcements were moved from their base in International Settlement to the Japanese lines and after a preliminary aerial reconnaissance which satisfied Japanese that Chinese had not evacuated their lines in conformity with demands Japanese opened attack on the 20th at 7:30 a.m. in Kiangwan and Woosung areas.”
It is important to note that the Japanese reenforcements were moved from bases in International Settlement.
Repeated to the Legation and Nanking for information.
